PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-23-2023 Continued To Nov-13-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Davy Borja in propria persona.

The court stated that it is not sure if Mr. Borja has made any progress and noted that a proposed order was not received. Mr. Borja stated that documents have been sent in and arrived to the court house today, that he sent out letters to all of the applicable heirs (himself, his sister and his cousin Michael, that he is the grandson of the decedent and the son of Linda, that Frank and Linda are the decedent's children, and that the uncle and his mother are deceased. Mr. Borja also stated that the uncle had one son, that he gave the son notice twice, that the decedent only had one property located at 109 Lowell Street, that he is trying to do this himself because he doesn't have money for a lawyer, and that they wanted to keep the property, but they may need to sell it because there are a number of violations and a large amount of damage to the property. The court informed Mr. Borja of the San Francisco Bar Association Referral Panel and provided him with the phone number. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to November 13, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Bastain. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-18-2023 Continued To Oct-23-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Davy Leo Borja (pro per) appeared in person (administrator).

The Court addressed the issues raised in the Probate Examiner's notes, that if he does not address the issues the petition will be taken off calendar. Mr. Borja stated that he will email the examiner and have the items at issue addressed before the next hearing date. Petition for letters of administration is continued to October 23, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Cecilia Castellanos. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Saephan. Not reported.
